trail.val: HBM ASP guided +40-50% YoY in 2026; pricing 'finalized', tracking start-of-year expectations; HBM4 ~35% more expensive than HBM3E
HBM4 ~35% pricier than HBM3E; Hynix HBM op margins flat (not -20% feared) — customers absorbing the hike

forward.note: Pricing locked higher; debate is whether buyers keep absorbing into 2027.

trail.val: HBM4 mass production in Sept; ships Q4
>70% of 2026 HBM bits still HBM3; HBM4 does NOT cross over in 2026 — blended <=50% of mix, ramping 2H26

forward.note: HBM4 ramps 2H26 but stays minority of 2026 mix.

trail.val: Cannot increase non-NVDA above 20% in 2026; declined ASIC/AMD volume; NVDA nudging Samsung to ramp
UBS models 2026 HBM share SK Hynix/Samsung/Micron = 56%/22%/22%, off historical ~90%
Dominant HBM supplier, largest HBM wafer cap ~200K end-2026 (vs Samsung 180K, Micron 100K); risk Samsung overtakes as #1 in 2027

Ray Wang/SemiAnalysis: in a fully-sold-out HBM market, Samsung gaining HBM share need NOT come at SK Hynix's expense — the bottleneck is wafer capacity, not demand; undersupplied through 2027 into 2028

forward.note: Share normalizing off ~90%; the '2027 #1 contested' bear is reframed as a capacity-constraint, not a zero-sum share fight.

trail.val: Exits 2026 #2 DRAM at ~610K wafers/month (vs Samsung ~740K); reaches 710K by end-2027, narrowing gap (Samsung adds only ~45K)

forward.note: Clean-room space — not equipment/demand — is the binding constraint through end-2028.

trail.val: Capex up 'significantly YoY'; M15X, Yongin, Indiana build-out
Yongin FAB1 (200-250K, geometry-dependent) ready end-2027, chip output only 2028; FAB2 just announced, 'far along'

Yongin 'Y1' pulled forward: Phase-1 pilot ('one-pass') line to Feb-2027 (from May), full install ~20K wpm Mar/Apr, targeting 1c DRAM (also HBM4E); ~$26.5bn ADR raise funds Korean capacity + more ASML EUV; 4th Yongin fab pulled forward 12yrs to 2033

forward.note: Greenfield clusters land output in 2028; the mega-plan/ADR raise accelerates the Yongin ramp. CEO Kwak: 2027 'the worst supply year in history', demand > capacity past 2030.

trail.val: 2027 HBM supply revised DOWN (60->50bn Gb-eq) as conventional DRAM more profitable; LTAs treated as call options; wafer shortage

forward.note: Hynix electing more commodity DRAM over HBM where margins favor it.
